title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Can I Take Out a Loan on My Life Insurance?
Keywords: loan against life insurance policy, loan against policy, loan against hdfc life policy, policy loan
Meta Description: Thinking about taking a loan against your life insurance policy? Read on to find out if it’s a good option to consider.
Your life insurance policy can not only help secure the financial future of your family but can also come in handy when you need immediate financial assistance in the present. In other words, when you need a sizable cash fund to meet a financial emergency, you can opt for a loan against your life insurance policy.
However, not all life insurance plans come with a loan against policy option. You can only get a policy loan on a whole life or permanent insurance plan that comes with a cash value that builds interest during your lifetime. Policies like term plans do not have a cash value because they expire after the set policy tenure, without earning any returns.
How Does a Life Insurance Loan Work?
Life insurance plans –like whole life and endowment plans- come with a cash value benefit that acts as collateral when you apply for a loan against your policy. However, you can opt for a policy loan only after the cash value of your life insurance policy reaches a certain minimum limit. This baseline limit varies from one insurer to the next, thus, to know the exact limit and loan application procedure for your policy, you must contact your insurer.
When Should I Opt for a Policy Loan?
You can never know when a financial emergency arises. For instance, you may require a large sum of money for immediate home renovations. In such a situation, a policy loan may be a better-suited option, since a bank loan may take a while to be sanctioned.
Typically, to qualify for a bank loan, you need to have a good CIBIL score. Similarly, bank loans take a long processing time and also come with high interest rates. Alternatively, loans against life insurance policies have a more abridged process of scrutiny that doesn’t take your credit score into account. The approval process for such loans is also more streamlined. This is because you’re essentially borrowing money from yourself when you apply for such a loan. Moreover, lower interest rates associated with policy loans also make them a popular credit option.
If you don’t have the collateral needed to meet bank loan requirements, opting for a policy loan may be a better option for you. Additionally, you also get to repay a policy loan without adhering to a strict repayment tenure that’s mandated for a bank loan. Thus, this gives you a lot of repayment flexibility.
Why You Shouldn’t Take Out A Life Insurance Loan?
After your demise, the death benefit payout of your life insurance policy helps your dependents meet their financial expenses, pay bills, mortgages, etc. However, if you take a loan against your policy and unfortunately pass away before it is repaid, your insurer will deduct the remaining amount from your death benefit payout. This will reduce the payout and thus, compromise the financial safety net you had intended to create for your family.
Therefore, a policy loan is only a good option if you are sure that you’ll be able to repay the loan over time. If you fail to do so, your policy cover may even lapse. This happens when the interest payments accumulate over time, amounting to a higher value than your policy’s original cash value.
Alternatives To Life Insurance Loans
If you wish to avoid putting your death benefit and overall policy cover in jeopardy, you can look into the following alternatives to a policy loan:
Opt for a bank loan: If you have a good credit score and eligible collateral, opting for a bank loan may be more prudent.
Cashing out your policy: Instead of applying for a policy loan, you can surrender your policy to reap its surrender value. However, this can only be done after you’ve paid a certain number of premiums.
What Documents Do You Need To Have When Applying For A Loan Against Your Insurance Policy?
While your insurer may have a slightly different list of document requirements, generally, you will need the following documents when applying for a loan against your policy:
·         A loan application form- For instance, if you’re applying for a loan against your HDFC life policy, you will need to get a loan application form from the insurer to begin the process.
·         Original insurance policy- You will also need to present your policy document in original.
·         Deed of assignment- As the policyholder, you will have to sign a deed of assignment that signifies you giving your policy benefits to the lender until the loan repayment is completed.
Summing it Up
A loan against your life insurance policy may be a good option for those looking to fund an immediate cash requirement, without the hassle of credit checks and arduous waiting periods that usually accompany traditional lending methods. However, before you opt for such a loan, remember that you stand to reduce the death benefit payout of the policy that’s designed to secure the financial future of your loved ones. There are alternatives to a policy loan and you should look into them to avoid compromising the benefits you stand to derive from the policy.
